2603.28304.title: The Necessity of Setting Temperature in LLM-as-a-Judge
2603.28304.paper_summary: LLM-as-a-Judge has emerged as an effective and low-cost paradigm for evaluating text quality and factual correctness. Prior studies have shown substantial agreement between LLM judges and human experts, even on tasks that are difficult to assess automatically. In practice, researchers commonly employ fixed temperature configurations during the evaluation process-with values of 0.1 and 1.0 being the most prevalent choices-a convention that is largely empirical rather than principled. However, recent researches suggest that LLM performance exhibits non-trivial sensitivity to temperature settings, that lower temperatures do not universally yield optimal outcomes, and that such effects are highly task-dependent. This raises a critical research question: does temperature influence judge performance in LLM centric evaluation? To address this, we systematically investigate the relationship between temperature and judge performance through a series of controlled experiments, and further adopt a causal inference framework within our empirical statistical analysis to rigorously examine the direct causal effect of temperature on judge behavior, offering actionable engineering insights for the design of LLM-centric evaluation pipelines.
2603.28304.paper_summary_zh: LLM-as-a-Judge 已經成為評估文本質量和事實正確性的一種有效且低成本的範式。<lb/>先前的研究顯示，LLM 評審與人類專家之間存在相當大的一致性，即使在難以自動評估的任務上也是如此。<lb/>在實踐中，研究人員通常在評估過程中使用固定的溫度配置——0.1 和 1.0 的值是最常見的選擇——這種慣例在很大程度上是經驗性的，而非原則性的。<lb/>然而，最近的研究表明，LLM 的表現對溫度設置顯示出非平凡的敏感性，較低的溫度並不總是產生最佳結果，並且這種影響高度依賴於任務。<lb/>這引發了一個關鍵的研究問題：溫度是否會影響 LLM 中心評估的評審表現？<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們通過一系列受控實驗系統地調查溫度與評審表現之間的關係，並在我們的實證統計分析中進一步採用因果推斷框架，以嚴格檢驗溫度對評審行為的直接因果影響，為 LLM 中心評估管道的設計提供可行的工程見解。
2603.28304.author: Lujun Li et.al.
2603.28304.authors: Lujun Li, Lama Sleem, Yangjie Xu, Yewei Song, Aolin Jia, Jerome Francois, Radu State
